City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Includes $1–1.5M temporary relocation costs during construction. $1.5M already set aside in free cash.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Rough preliminary estimate. Current Park Street facility is nearly 100 years old and cannot be expanded. Site has not yet been identified.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Six elementary schools plus the high school. Roofs from 1996–2003 renovations are approaching end of useful life. MSBA reimbursement possible but uncertain.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Construction began FY2025. Funded through ARPA funds, state and federal earmarks. No additional free cash or debt planned.

metric: Beverly Golf and Tennis Club renovation
source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Major systems overhaul. Intended to be funded through Golf and Tennis revenues, CPA funds, and grants, but full cost likely exceeds what the enterprise fund can support.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Two-phase reconstruction. Funding mix: state historic preservation grants, CPA funds, Recreation Enterprise Fund, and General Fund debt.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Window and door replacement, weatherization, and full HVAC replacement. Expected within 2–4 years.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Combination of city appropriation, free cash, gas tax revenue, and grants (Complete Streets, MassWorks).

metric: North Beverly and Beverly Farms fire station roofs
source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Roof replacements planned within 5 years. Heat pump installation to follow.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: New Ferrara pumper ordered in FY2024. Engine 3 replacement anticipated around FY2027.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Ongoing annual equipment replacement: one-ton trucks, medium-duty trucks, front-end loaders, backhoes.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Dispatch and radio infrastructure serving police, fire, and DPW. Many components no longer manufacturer-supported.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Serves all city vehicles. Work going to bid.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Facade restoration completed; paint showing wear.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Dunham Road / Rte. 128 interchange design approved at $850K in FY2024. Brimbal/Dunham roundabout construction estimated at $7M.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Downtown and waterfront lots. $2M BAN outstanding on recently purchased Bow/Federal St. lot.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Fencing replacement and Cole Street cemetery buildout. Three active cemeteries nearing capacity.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Two-phase project. Significant logistical challenges; feasibility study underway for ocean disposal site.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Seawall and shoreline work at Lynch Park, Obear Park, and Dane Street Beach. Grant funding being pursued.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Replacement vehicle needed within 5–10 years.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: All city water meters are over 20 years old. New system enables real-time leak detection. Requires separate loan authorization.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: DEP-mandated work on 25 sewer subsections. Current annual appropriation is $1.7M, below the required pace.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Key sewer line runs under Dane and Independence Park beaches. High priority: failure consequences are severe.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Interior and exterior recoating needed within 5 years. Debt obligation of the water fund.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Beverly has about 200 miles of water pipe. Industry standard is to replace 1% per year.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: 35 stations in operation. Two stations need immediate overhaul; $400K appropriation likely needed.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Ongoing equipment replacement for water and sewer operations.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Nearly 4,000 valves in the water system, some over 100 years old. About 50 replacements per year at $4,000 each.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Building envelope repairs complete. Pumping equipment nearing end of service life; design estimate in progress.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Federally mandated stormwater quality program. Full cost still being scoped by consultants.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Small drainage improvement projects constructed by DPS crews.

source: City of Beverly 2025 Draft Capital Expenditure Plan, submitted by Mayor Michael P. Cahill to City Council, January 30, 2025
note: Clearing and maintaining brooks in flood-prone areas.
